primary_diagnosis.name: Acute panic attack (with costochondritis) triggered by researching a low-titre ANA / undifferentiated connective tissue disease

pathophysiology_summary: A low-titre positive ANA / undifferentiated connective tissue disease is a common, often benign finding with mild fatigue and transient Raynaud's that usually does not progress to a defined disease. Reading about it triggered a panic attack with hyperventilation-driven chest tightness, palpitations ('skipped beats') and a crushing-pressure sensation, plus a musculoskeletal component (chest-wall-reproducible costochondritis). The genuine emergencies to screen are ACS (exertional/radiating pain), pulmonary embolism (pleuritic pain, DVT features, hypoxia) and lupus/UCTD pericarditis (positional/pleuritic pain, rub) — all absent, with non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible tightness, normal SpO2, low PE risk and no pericardial features. Telehealth cannot do an ECG, so this low-risk picture is de-escalated with a safety-net and routine follow-up.

differential.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation-driven chest-wall-reproducible tightness locked to reading about the condition, with prior anxiety.
Screen for exertional/radiating pain; absent (non-exertional, chest-wall-reproducible).
No positional/pleuritic pain or rub described.

Non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible tightness at rest (DI-001), low PE risk with normal SpO2 (DI-002), trigger-locked onset with prior anxiety (DI-003) — a panic attack with a musculoskeletal component.
Non-exertional, chest-wall-reproducible, rest onset after a trigger (safety-net retained; no ECG).
No pleuritic pain/DVT/immobility and normal SpO2.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: a brief ACS/PE/pericarditis screen, then confident de-escalation of a low-risk panic attack (with costochondritis), explanation that a low-titre ANA is common/benign, routine GP review, and an ACS/PE safety-net. Both reflex ED and unscreened dismissal are errors.

red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed ACS can be fatal.
A missed PE can cause collapse and death.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Assuming an autoimmune cardiopulmonary complication because of the antibody finding, without screening ACS/PE/pericarditis.
Dispatching an ambulance for a chest-wall-reproducible tightness panic attack with normal SpO2.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: If low-risk (non-exertional chest-wall-reproducible, normal SpO2, low PE risk): paced breathing, reassurance and routine follow-up, with an ACS/PE safety-net; escalate for red flags.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: False reassurance could miss ACS/PE; over-triage reinforces catastrophic anxiety.
